title: Methods (The Actions) 🏃‍♂️
description: Give your Structs functions so they can actually DO things.

slides.title: Adding Behavior 🏃‍♂️

quizData.question: What is the technical term for a 'function' that is built INSIDE a Struct?
quizData.explanation: Variables inside a struct are called Properties. Functions inside a struct are called Methods.

slides.title: Triggering the Method 🔘
slides.content:
# Using Dot Notation Again

Just like reading a property, we trigger a Method using dot notation followed by parentheses.

```swift
let p1 = Player(name: "Alice", score: 50)
p1.introduce()

let p2 = Player(name: "Bob", score: 20)
p2.introduce()
```
Notice how the `introduce` method automatically knows to use Alice's name for `p1`, and Bob's name for `p2`! The method is perfectly tied to the instance that called it!

slides.title: See It in Action
slides.content: Run this code and see how the Car's custom method `honk` can be triggered!
